scope: Exact evaluated topology, near-identical local positions and normals, and authored Blender viewport material parity.

geometry.surfaceComparison: Blender evaluation exported with tools/export_blender_evaluated_mesh.py and checked with tools/mesh-surface-diff.ts --local
geometry.normalComparison: Blender evaluated vertex normals exported by tools/export_blender_evaluated_mesh.py and compared by identical vertex index to GN-VM TriSoup normals

material.name: chrome.001
material.graph: Direct Principled BSDF to active Material Output
material.browserImplementation: Generic makeBasicBlenderMaterial constant-Principled path; no asset-specific shader is used.

capture.rotationInterpretation: The per-asset longitude compensates for Blender/Three environment parameterization on this reflective local-space surface. It does not rotate or alter the generated mesh.

interpretation: All evaluated faces use the recovered polished chrome.001 Principled surface. Exact triangle indices, sub-micro-unit indexed position error, and sub-0.012-degree maximum normal error isolate the remaining image difference to Eevee/WebGL PBR and environment-filtering behavior. The measured studio calibration matches mean exposure within 0.0016 and retains positive broad-scale reflection correlation without changing geometry.
